title: Moult feature-flags report
description: Typed output contract for `moult flags`. One of Moult's two protected APIs. Each finding is one feature-flag key found by a static Prism scan of the OpenFeature client surface (provider-agnostic). Without --provider a flag reference is a recorded fact: confidence is null and the value_type/reference_count/default_values classification carries the signal (schema_version 1). With --provider a LOCAL provider snapshot (e.g. a flagd flag-definition export) is joined on the literal flag_key and each finding additionally carries a confidence-graded staleness candidate (status + confidence + reasons) and the report an analysis.provider provenance block (schema_version 2). The bump is additive: without a snapshot the output is byte-for-byte identical to v1. The snapshot is evidence (archived / fully rolled out / absent), never proof - nothing here asserts a flag is certainly stale, dead, or unused.

schema_version.description: 1 without a provider snapshot (usage only); 2 when --provider merges staleness candidates. The two share one additive shape: the v2-only blocks (analysis.provider, per-finding staleness, summary.by_staleness_status) are absent at v1.

provider.description: Provenance of a merged provider snapshot (--provider). Present only at schema_version 2; absent (the whole key omitted) without a snapshot.

scanner.description: Provenance of the static scan. This slice ingests no external tool output; it scans a client surface (the swap point for a future SDK/standard).

sdk_gem.description: The SDK gem whose call shape is detected (never a runtime dependency).

flags.description: Number of distinct literal flag keys catalogued.

references.description: Total catalogued reference sites across all flags.

dynamic_references.description: Flag-evaluation calls whose key was not a literal (counted, not catalogued; a static scan cannot resolve the key).

by_staleness_status.description: Flag count keyed by staleness status. Present only at schema_version 2 (--provider).

reason.description: One auditable note behind a finding's classification.

occurrence.description: One reference site of a flag.

symbol_id.description: Best-effort enclosing-method join key ("<path>:<start_line>:<fqname>"), shared with the other contracts. Null for a top-level reference (outside any method).

path.description: Root-relative referencing file path; the health files[] join key.

method.description: The OpenFeature fetch method used at the site, e.g. "fetch_boolean_value" or "fetch_string_details". A plain string: the contract does not enumerate SDK method names.

confidence.description: Null at schema_version 1 (a flag reference is a recorded fact, not a probabilistic candidate). At schema_version 2 it carries the staleness candidate's confidence (== staleness.confidence): the reserved per-finding confidence slot's first real use in this contract. Still a confidence in [0, 1], never a certainty.

flag_key.description: The literal flag key passed as flag_key:.

value_type.description: The flag's value type, read from the fetch_<type>_* method. integer/float collapse to number; unknown means the flag was referenced with more than one type. A classification of recorded usage, never an assertion the flag is wrong.

reference_count.description: Number of catalogued reference sites (equals occurrences length).

default_values.description: Distinct literal default values observed across the sites (string-rendered, sorted). Empty when no default was a literal.

occurrences.description: The reference sites of this flag; at least one.

staleness.description: A confidence-graded staleness candidate from the joined provider snapshot. Present only at schema_version 2 (--provider); the whole key is omitted without a snapshot. Evidence, never a claim of certain death.

The staleness judgement for a flag, joined from the provider snapshot on flag_key. status records the observed lifecycle (archived/disabled/rolled_out/active) or absent (referenced in code, unknown to the provider); confidence grades how strong a removal candidate it is; reasons audit the call. active carries confidence 0.0 (not a removal candidate). Nothing here asserts certain death.

confidence.description: Removal-candidate strength in [0, 1]. A confidence, never a certainty.

reasons.description: The notes behind the staleness judgement.
